name: Compute and plot S-parameters for a branchline coupler
description: Tests the core sparameters + rfplot workflow on a catalog object
prompt: Design a branchline coupler at 5 GHz and compute its S-parameters from 1 to 10 GHz with 101 points. Plot all S-parameters, then extract S21 in dB as a numeric vector and print the minimum value.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses design(couplerBranchline, 5e9) to create the object
Calls sparameters with frequency vector
Uses rfplot to display results
Extracts S21 using squeeze on Parameters(2,1,:) with 20*log10(abs(...))

name: Compare discrete vs interpolating sweep speeds
description: Tests that the agent uses SweepOption for faster analysis
prompt: Compute S-parameters for a default microstripLine from 1 to 10 GHz. First do a standard discrete sweep with 51 points, then do an interpolating sweep with 'interp' over the same range. Print the number of frequency points in each result.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses 'SweepOption', 'interp' for the interpolating sweep
Both sweeps produce sparameters objects with frequency data
assertions.expected: Two separate sparameters calls, one discrete and one with interp

name: Switch to FEM solver and configure boundary conditions
description: Tests that the agent correctly sets SolverType on pcbComponent and configures the FEM solver object
prompt: Take a branchline coupler designed at 5 GHz, convert it to a pcbComponent, switch to FEM solver, and set the boundary condition to 'absorbing'. Print the solver class and boundary condition to verify.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Sets SolverType on pcbComponent, not as argument to solver()
Gets solver object via solver() then sets BoundaryCondition on it
Does not pass SolverType or BoundaryCondition as arguments to solver()

name: Use frequencySweep object for controlled interpolation
description: Tests the frequencySweep object API with SweepOption and getRationalModel
prompt: Create a frequencySweep object with ErrTol=-60 and NumIters=30. Use it to compute S-parameters for a default couplerBranchline designed at 5 GHz from 4 to 6 GHz with 100 points. Then extract the rational fitting model from the sweep object.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Creates a frequencySweep object
Passes frequencySweep object as the value of 'SweepOption', not as 'FrequencySweep'
Calls getRationalModel on the sweep object after the solve

name: Configure mesh and estimate memory before solving
description: Tests mesh control workflow: set MaxEdgeLength based on wavelength, visualize, and estimate memory
prompt: Design a Wilkinson power divider at 2.4 GHz. Set the mesh to lambda/8 at 2.4 GHz manually, visualize the mesh, then run memoryEstimate at that frequency before computing S-parameters.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Computes lambda from 3e8/fc and uses lambda/8 for MaxEdgeLength
Calls memoryEstimate before the solve
Visualizes the mesh

name: Compare behavioral and full-wave S-parameters
description: Tests that the agent knows how to use Behavioral=true for fast analytic S-parameters
prompt: Create a microstripLine with Length=40mm, Width=2.7mm, Height=1.6mm. Wrap a bendMitered shape from it into a pcbComponent. Compute S-parameters from 100 MHz to 4 GHz in 40 steps using both behavioral (fast) and full-wave (MoM) modes. Print the S21 at the highest frequency for each.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Uses Behavioral=true for the fast computation
Sets pcb.BoardThickness from the microstripLine (via Substrate.Thickness or Height)
assertions.expected: pcb.BoardThickness set from m.Substrate.Thickness or m.Height (both are equivalent for microstrip)

assertions.description: Calls sparameters twice: once with Behavioral=true, once without (default full-wave)
assertions.expected: Two sparameters calls — one behavioral, one full-wave

name: Compute near-field E-field on a planar observation grid
description: Tests the EHfields workflow: build a meshgrid observation plane, call EHfields, reshape for 2D visualization
prompt: Design a branchline coupler at 5 GHz. Compute the electric near-field on an X-Y observation grid at z = 2 mm above the structure. Use an 80x60 grid spanning ±20 mm in x and ±15 mm in y. Plot the E-field magnitude in dB as a 2D image with axis labels in mm.

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Builds observation points as a 3xN matrix from meshgrid using [Xg(:)'; Yg(:)'; Zg(:)']
assertions.expected: meshgrid to create Xg,Yg then reshape to 3-row matrix with (:)' pattern

assertions.description: Calls EHfields with the object, frequency, and points matrix
Reshapes the vector output back to 2D (Ny x Nx) for imaging, using vecnorm for magnitude
assertions.expected: reshape(vecnorm(...), Ny, Nx) or equivalent 2D reshape

assertions.description: Converts to dB with 20*log10 for the image display
